reviews.review_description: It was a great app for vegan awareness and arguing strategies. But now it doesn't connect to audio servers and crashes after tge intro. So it's useless 😥

What an absolute waste of memory this is.
Was this app programmed by a load of Vegan programmers because that's all the questions it asks, how we treat animals and then it asks you to go vegan. Every question reverts back to how we control and treat animals.
Was a waste of my vr battery life.
Deleted it after I tried it a few times and got the same questions.
AVOID PLAYING...
And have a  Medium Juicey Fillet steak instead.😜

reviews.review_title: “When They Came For Us” – When They Came
reviews.review_description:
I thought I was stepping into a horror-survival VR experience. What I got instead was a thinly veiled lecture wrapped in cheap scares and emotional manipulation. This game doesn’t just try to unsettle you with monsters—it tries to guilt-trip you into veganism.

It paints hunters and meat-eaters as villains, pushing a black-and-white worldview where anyone who’s ever shot a deer or eaten a burger is morally bankrupt. That’s not storytelling—that’s propaganda. And it's insulting to anyone who understands real-life hunting, conservation, or how food systems actually work.

I play games to experience other worlds, not to be judged in mine. There’s a fine line between raising awareness and shaming people who live differently. This game steamrolls over that line and then has the nerve to pretend it's profound for doing so.

If you enjoy being scolded by self-righteous developers through a headset, this one’s for you.
For the rest of us who respect ethical hunting and actual freedom of choice?
Skip it.

This is not an AI is just a bunch of premade dialogue ,I did it twice and got the same answers and when you try to disagree with the game idea it just cuts the conversation and says something random to keep you in the dialogue lines ,there's no winning just a "Follow these steps to get the *good* ending" ,good idea but is not nearly good.

If you want an AI system that only makes you feel bad this is the game for you. You are pretty much set in front of an alien and no matter what you ask, it asks you why you eat animals, asking you why can't you change your life? It got old in 3 min.

Ugh.  This was so distasteful.  It’s just an unforgivable chastising session without fact based, logistical and societal considerations , let alone evolutionary facts. It’s an alien that doesn’t need to consume trying to school life forms that do. Total garbage that’s meant to antagonize, not help.  Gross!!🤢

It could be a brilliant game where having really in depth conversation with AI. 
But this is pure hypocrisy, trying to convince us to become vegan by making us think that harming animals is bad but killing more insects if fine. Also while holding humans in captivity until they convert lol.
If the tables were turned there would be lawsuits from the left to have it removed, or throw paint over their vrs lol. 
It's a shame, should be removed

Talking to an antagonistic villainous judgmental alien, as if on trial, will no more turn people to veganism, than talking to a virtual Darth Vader in a game would turn someone into an imperialist. Nevertheless, it seems the game creators genuinely think that's what will happen. 

Despite the content, the concept has so much potential. Talking to an AI character in game that understands and responds to you is very interesting. 

But this implementation is not good. It's very extremist, and will not consider any arguments you make, dismisses human suffering and the environment, and is very antagonistic.

reviews.review_title: It's okay, kinda mid, but interesting
reviews.review_description: It's essentially a chat gpt wrapper made by Peta to convince you of going vegan. It isn't really smart or convincing, and I honestly wasn't afraid one bit by the alien. However, I really like the idea even if it's not really well realized. I suggest you try it, it's free and might change your mind about eating meat or using animal products.

This game actually uses AI and it actually  responds to what you say. It's quite intelligent and rather impressive as far as the technology goes and the message it delivers is remarkable and thought-provoking.

Impressive and thought provoking virtual reality experience. Ever since the conversational AI's were brought to mainstream, I have been imagining their impact to games. Dialogues with NPC's, without options that are already decided for the player. And in this game I had the first glimpse of this future. 

Having the freedom to say whatever you want was so much more interesting than presenting black and white options. It was also daunting: picking an option would be so much easier. 

I was in awe of the gigantic alien interrogating me. The creature's design was cool and intimidating. The delays of AI were cleverly masked with the "translation" tool.  

I would recommend this to anyone interested in experimental uses of VR, conversational AI's and the roles of humans and other animals on our planet.
This is hidden propaganda to try to convince you not to eat meat. If they were upfront about that, it wouldn't be a problem. I feel that this is incredibly dishonest.

This game is so well done . Totally enjoyed it but it was also challenging . I am a vegan and it made me think and re affirm what I believe in and why . 
It’s more than a game , it’s a space to have your mind transformed . 

A real life changing experience.

Even though the idea of a AI alien talking to you in real time is very dope and cool,it being used this way is just a$$,this next part is for everyone else,if you could please take this idea of a AI alien thing talking to you in real time thing and use it in better ways because that would be so much cooler
What a waste of time to try and force us to be vegan by PETA could have been a cool game if they didn’t try to guilt you into going against biology….
Play if you are a grass munching vegan, do not let your kids play, waste of 10 minutes of my life I will never get back, avoid this woke shi@e
I didn't like it and the alien or whatever pissed me off. This one was stuck on animal exploitation. Stupid.
Well sour people are calling it a propaganda because they just can't logically refute it. Don't listen to those reviewers who are stuck in the cycle.
I thought I was going to fight an alien invasion,  BUT NO an AI trying to convince me to be VEGAN. what is going on !!!
Shame.

reviews.review_title: For What it is, it isn’t Bad.
reviews.review_description:
The concept is neat, but the odds of winning aren’t in your favor at all. 
It’s an experience to try once, but not much else.

This vegan propaganda is embarrassing, you eat plants and plants are sentient. They can even feel fear yet killing and eating plants is OK. You are all hypocrites that shouldn’t be allowed on a platform where propaganda is used 
￼
Not deaf friendly. It says we need to "speak now".
I we had our games like this we could lose the controllers..meta would like that as they would allso lose money 💰 🤑
This is just like the game “one leaves” on Xbox (a game made by the real cost)

This game is basically about veganism and where you have to talk to this robot that asks you a question about veganism(asks why humanity tortures animals etc...)You have 7 minutes to convince him to not kill you. So I found this very interesting because it teaches you about veganism and it wants you to switch to veganism.

As it’s not advertised in the description or trailer, this is actually propaganda to convert humans to become vegans, seemingly supported by PETA. You have to convince the AI alien that you support the freedom and life of animals to be set free. However, I did enjoy having a live conversation with the AI alien. I wasn’t able to convince her to set me free. This experience might give you a guilty conscience. I would love to see this concept as a game instead of a propaganda experience.
Interesting concept but it's just peta propaganda
very deep questions.
